seed_instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals.
org_constraint: The email must consist of exactly three paragraphs.
new_constraint: The email should consist of 10 sentences, with each paragraph containing at least four sentences.
instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals. The email must consist of exactly three paragraphs. The email should consist of 10 sentences, with each paragraph containing at least four sentences.
response:
Subject: Introduction - [Your Name] - Aspiring Student in [Professor's Field]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name], my name is [Your Name] and I am a highly motivated [Your Year, e.g., sophomore/graduate] student interested in your work on [Specific area of the professor's research]. I have been following your recent publications regarding [Mention a specific paper or topic] with great interest. Your insights into [Specific concept] particularly resonated with my own academic curiosity. I am eager to delve deeper into these fascinating aspects of [Professor's Field].

My academic goals are strongly aligned with the research conducted in your lab and department. Specifically, I aim to pursue advanced studies in [Specific area of study] after completing my undergraduate coursework. I am particularly passionate about [Mention a specific problem or area you want to solve], and I believe your guidance would be invaluable to this pursuit. I am committed to developing a strong theoretical foundation while gaining hands-on research experience.

I would be grateful for the opportunity to briefly discuss my interests and how I might contribute to your ongoing projects. Would you be available for a short virtual meeting sometime in the coming weeks? Thank you very much for considering my introduction and for your time. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID or Contact Information]<turn|>
